target.title: feat(plugins): add guard delivery and subagent review hooks
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the current hook surface can gate tool execution, but it cannot synchronously guard tool results before the agent sees them and it has no explicit pre-spawn / pre-return subagent review phases.
- Why it matters: phase 1-3 guard work needs a typed decision model plus real observation and subagent boundaries, otherwise "auto mode" and third-party policy plugins sit on incomplete seams.
- What changed: added shared guard decision/annotation types, new `before_tool_result_deliver` / `before_subagent_spawn` / `before_subagent_result_deliver` hooks, wired them into tool-result persistence and subagent spawn/announce flows, and extended targeted tests.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not add a guard kernel, model-backed policy runtime, or any bundled automode/firewall plugin yet.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): N/A
- Why this regressed now: N/A
- If unknown, what was ruled out: N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/plugins/wired-hooks-subagent.test.ts`, `src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.tool-result-persist-hook.test.ts`, `src/agents/sessions-spawn-hooks.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-announce.test.ts`, `src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.integration.e2e.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: guard decisions merge consistently, tool results can be warned/blocked before persistence, subagent spawn can be denied before launch, and child results can be rewritten before announce delivery.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: these tests hit the exact hook seams without needing the full policy stack.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing hook wiring tests cover the older lifecycle hooks only.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Plugins can now return typed guard decisions and annotations from `before_tool_call`.
- Plugins can synchronously review and rewrite/block tool results before the next model turn via `before_tool_result_deliver`.
- Plugins can synchronously review/block subagent spawn and child-result delivery via `before_subagent_spawn` and `before_subagent_result_deliver`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[tool call] -> [before_tool_call] -> [execute] -> [tool_result_persist] -> [agent sees result]
[subagent spawn] -> [subagent_spawning] -> [child runs] -> [subagent announce]

After:
[tool call] -> [before_tool_call] -> [execute] -> [before_tool_result_deliver] -> [tool_result_persist] -> [agent sees guarded result]
[subagent spawn] -> [before_subagent_spawn] -> [subagent_spawning] -> [child runs] -> [before_subagent_result_deliver] -> [subagent announce]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: new hook phases can block or rewrite execution/results, but they only extend existing plugin authority. Mitigation is typed decision shapes, synchronous ordering, and targeted seam coverage around the new hook paths.

## Repro + Verification

2. Run the corresponding tool/subagent flow.

### Actual

- Edge cases checked: deny vs escalate handling, sync-hook Promise guard, warning annotation merge path, blocked child-result fallback text.

target.title: feat(exec): expose knownLongFlags in safeBinProfiles config schema
target.body:
## Summary

Expose the `knownLongFlags` field in the `safeBinProfiles` config schema, allowing operators to declare long flags as "known" for GNU abbreviation resolution without misusing `allowedValueFlags` or `deniedFlags`.

Closes #56805

> **Rebased 2026-06-10.** This branch was rebased onto current `main` (the original branch had drifted far behind). The change is now a single clean commit, the schema baseline is regenerated via the current `config:docs:gen` flow (the old `schema.base.generated.ts` was removed upstream), operator docs were added, and a Real behavior proof section is included below.

## Problem

The `knownLongFlags` field exists internally in `SafeBinProfile` and is used by `validateSafeBinArgv` for GNU long-option abbreviation resolution. However, it is not present in:

1. The Zod schema (`ToolExecSafeBinProfileSchema`) — `.strict()` rejects it as an unknown key
2. The `SafeBinProfileFixture` type — no way to pass it through config normalization

For empty profiles `{}`, `knownLongFlags` auto-derives as `[]`, causing **all** long flags to be treated as unknown and rejected. The only workaround is to list every possible long flag in `allowedValueFlags` or `deniedFlags`, which changes their semantics.

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags: z.array(z.string()).optional()` to `ToolExecSafeBinProfileSchema` |
| `src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy-profiles.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags` to `SafeBinProfileFixture`; normalize it in `normalizeSafeBinProfileFixture`; merge user-provided with auto-derived flags in `compileSafeBinProfile`, filtering strict prefixes of denied flags |
| `src/config/normalize-exec-safe-bin.ts` | Add `knownLongFlags` to the type cast in normalization |
| `docs/tools/exec-approvals-advanced.md` | Document the field, its auto-derivation default, and the prefix-filter security semantics |
| `docs/.generated/config-baseline.sha256` | Regenerated via `pnpm config:docs:gen` for the new field |
| `src/infra/exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts` | 8 new tests for the config path + security filter |

## Design Decisions

- **Merge, don't replace**: User-provided `knownLongFlags` are merged with auto-derived flags from `allowedValueFlags ∪ deniedFlags`, so flags in those lists stay "known" even if not explicitly listed.
- **Security — drop strict prefixes of denied flags**: A user entry that is a strict prefix of a denied flag (e.g. `--rec` when `--recursive` is denied) is filtered at compile time, so it cannot gain exact-match priority in `resolveCanonicalLongFlag` and bypass the denial. (Addresses the prior Codex review P1.)
- **Reuses `normalizeFixtureFlags`**: Same normalization (trim, dedupe, sort) as other flag arrays.
- **Backwards-compatible**: Existing configs without `knownLongFlags` work identically via auto-derivation.

## Usage

```json5
{
  tools: {
    exec: {
      safeBins: ["mycli"],
      safeBinProfiles: {
        mycli: {
          allowedValueFlags: ["--limit"],
          deniedFlags: ["--recursive"],
          knownLongFlags: ["--dry-run", "--verbose"],
        },
      },
    },
  },
}
```

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Setting `tools.exec.safeBinProfiles.<bin>.knownLongFlags` in a real config previously could not be accepted — the strict Zod schema had no such key. After this patch the field is accepted by config validation and is honored by the safe-bin argv validator (declared long flags resolve, including GNU abbreviation), while strict prefixes of denied flags are still blocked.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw build from this branch, `OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (bb6e477)`, Node v22.22.0, run via the real `openclaw` CLI and the compiled `exec-safe-bin-policy` module (not mocks).

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```
# 1) Real CLI accepts a config containing knownLongFlags
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/klf-proof.json5 openclaw config validate --json
# config: { tools: { exec: { safeBins: ["mycli"], safeBinProfiles: {
#   mycli: { allowedValueFlags: ["--limit"], deniedFlags: ["--recursive"],
#            knownLongFlags: ["--dry-run", "--verbose"] } } } } }

# 2) Same CLI still rejects an unknown key (the pre-fix state of knownLongFlags)
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/klf-proof-unknown.json5 openclaw config validate --json
#   ( mycli: { knownLongFlagsTYPO: ["--dry-run"] } )

# 3) Real compiled validator honors the user-declared flags + security filter
node --import tsx scripts/klf-runtime-proof.mts
```

**Evidence after fix:**

```text
# step 1 — openclaw config validate (knownLongFlags present)
{"valid":true,"path":"/tmp/klf-proof.json5"}

# step 2 — openclaw config validate (unknown key knownLongFlagsTYPO)
{"valid":false,"path":"/tmp/klf-proof-unknown.json5",
 "issues":[{"path":"tools.exec.safeBinProfiles.mycli","message":"Invalid input"}]}

# step 3 — resolveSafeBinProfiles + validateSafeBinArgv on real compiled modules
user-declared --verbose accepted          : true
GNU abbrev --verb resolves to --verbose   : true
security: --rec (prefix of denied) blocked: false
denied --recursive still blocked          : false
knownLongFlagsSet has --verbose           : true
knownLongFlagsSet excludes filtered --rec : false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The live `openclaw config validate` returns `valid: true` for a config carrying `knownLongFlags`, and still returns `valid: false` for an unknown sibling key — confirming the field is genuinely wired into the strict schema. At runtime the compiled validator accepts the user-declared `--verbose`, resolves the `--verb` abbreviation to it, keeps `--recursive` denied, and drops the `--rec` prefix so it cannot bypass that denial.

**What was not tested:** End-to-end exec of a real third-party binary through the approval pipeline (requires a trusted-dir executable); behavior is covered at the policy/validator layer instead.

## Test Plan

- [x] All tests in `exec-safe-bin-policy.test.ts` pass: **98 passed** (90 existing + 8 new) under `vitest.infra.config.ts`
- [x] `pnpm config:docs:check` and `pnpm config:schema:check` pass (baseline in sync, no drift)
- [x] `oxlint` clean on changed files; no new type errors introduced
- [x] Real behavior proof captured above via the `openclaw` CLI and compiled validator

target.title: fix(hooks): restore --exclude-labels in Gmail watcher args
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #56635

- Restores `--exclude-labels SPAM,TRASH,DRAFT,SENT` in `gog gmail watch serve` args so sent mail and draft saves do not trigger Gmail hook runs by default.
- Adds `hooks.gmail.excludeLabels` to the config schema/types with runtime default resolution and hook override support.
- Updates the sanitized log-args expectation so `--exclude-labels` appears in non-secret watcher logs.
- Regenerates the base config schema and docs baseline hash, and documents the default excluded label set.

target.title: feat(extension): support session cache for volcengine
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets: - Problem: Volcengine provider lacked session-based response caching support for multi-turn conversations. - Why it matters: Without session caching, every turn sends full context to the API, increasing latency and cost for Volcengine Doubao models. - What changed: Added extensions/volcengine/cache.ts with session cache logic — manages previous_response_id, caching, thinking, store, and expire_at fields automatically. Configured via params.cache: { enable: true, ttlSec?, thinking? }. Wired into the provider via wrapStreamFn in index.ts. (cache.test.ts, 27 tests). Updated docs with config examples. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): Provider registration, catalog, auth flow unchanged. No changes outside extensions/volcengine/ and docs. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [x] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [x] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # - [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). Once enable cache and openai-responses, we can see the cache read token from session file for each LLM request config example： ``` "models": { "providers": { "volcengine": { "api": "openai-responses" } } }, "agents": { "defaults": { "models": { "volcengine/xxxx": { "alias": "volcengine", "params": { "cache": { "enable": false, "ttlSec": 3600, "thinking": true } } } } } } ``` session file example: ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? target.title: fix(gateway): preserve indentation when recombining non-streaming replies
target.body:
AI-assisted: yes (OpenClaw agent-authored).
Testing: targeted local validation (gateway regression tests + targeted lint + pre-commit repo checks).

## Summary

- Companion to merged #55960, not a duplicate: #55960 fixed indentation loss while stripping inline reply directives in `src/utils/directive-tags.ts`; this PR fixes the remaining gateway-layer loss when non-streaming reply parts are recombined in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`.
- Preserve each non-streaming payload chunk as authored when recombining it, so indentation-sensitive content survives payload boundaries.
- Reuse that same combiner for `/btw` side results so sibling non-streaming gateway paths behave consistently.
- Normalize `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` boundary trimming and add focused regression coverage for the single-newline, `/btw`, and CRLF cases.
- Scope boundary: no streaming-path formatting changes, no Markdown parsing changes, and no channel-specific formatting rules.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #42576
- Related #29377
- Companion to merged #55960

## Relationship to #55960

Merged PR #55960 fixed one real whitespace-loss layer: inline reply-directive stripping in the shared parser (`src/utils/directive-tags.ts`).

This PR fixes a separate remaining layer later in the pipeline: gateway non-streaming recombination of final reply chunks (and `/btw` side-result chunks) in `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`.

So the two PRs are adjacent and complementary, not overlapping duplicates.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Non-streaming final replies and `/btw` side results now preserve indentation when formatted content spans multiple payloads. When one chunk already ends with a single newline and the next chunk does not start with one, the gateway now preserves that authored single newline instead of forcing a blank line. The combiner also trims `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` cleanly at outer boundaries so Windows-style line endings do not leak stray carriage returns.

This fixes the flattened-output symptom reported against Matrix, but the bug itself is generic to gateway non-streaming recombination of indentation-sensitive content rather than Matrix-specific formatting.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local Node.js checkout
- Model/provider: n/a (gateway-side reply recombination)
- Integration/channel (if any): reproduced against the gateway non-streaming `chat.send` path; user report was on Matrix
- Relevant config (redacted): n/a

### Steps

1. Produce a non-streaming assistant reply whose formatted block is emitted across multiple final payloads.
2. Include indentation-sensitive content such as nested YAML, a nested list, or a fenced code block.
3. Observe the stored/broadcast final message text.

### Expected

- Indentation from each payload chunk is preserved in the final combined reply.

### Actual

- Leading whitespace at payload boundaries was trimmed away, flattening structured content.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Added focused regression coverage for:
- the authored single-newline separator case
- an indentation-sensitive `/btw` YAML side result
- `CRLF` boundary trimming

The linked user issue includes before/after screenshots of the real-world symptom.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: targeted gateway regression test for the single-newline recombination edge case; targeted `/btw` side-result regression test for an indented YAML block; targeted `CRLF` boundary regression coverage; targeted lint on the changed files; repo pre-commit checks during commit.
- Edge cases checked: whitespace-only payload chunks are still ignored; separator insertion still only happens when adjacent chunks are not already separated by line breaks; authored single-newline separators are preserved instead of expanded to blank lines; `CRLF` boundaries do not leave stray carriage returns.
- What you did **not** verify: live Matrix delivery against a running instance, broader streaming reply behavior outside the existing `/btw` block-side-result coverage, or full-repo test suites.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: revert commits `6b2461a11`, `c753cd0af`, and `d667f18f1` (or revert the PR branch changes as a unit)
- Files/config to restore: `src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts`, and `src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: unexpected extra blank lines between merged payload chunks, unexpected loss of an authored single newline between chunks, stray carriage returns from `CRLF` boundaries, or lost indentation in `/btw` side results

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: preserving authored separators changes the old trim/join behavior for payloads that already end in a single newline.
  - Mitigation: this is intentional, explicitly regression-tested, and still bounded by the same whitespace-only-chunk skipping plus separator insertion only when neither side already provides a line break.
- Risk: preserving chunk text could expose `CRLF` edge cases at outer boundaries.
  - Mitigation: the combiner now trims `CRLF` / `CR` / `LF` consistently at the start/end, with targeted regression coverage.
- Risk: reviewers may mistake this PR for a duplicate of #55960.
  - Mitigation: this description now names the exact layer split: `#55960` fixes directive parsing, this PR fixes gateway non-streaming recombination.

target.title: feat(irc): support markdown messages via draft/multiline
target.body:
## Summary

Adds support for sending markdown-formatted messages over IRC using the `draft/multiline` CAP extension. This preserves newlines in bot responses, allowing IRC clients to properly render markdown content.

## Motivation

OpenClaw bots typically generate markdown-formatted responses (lists, code blocks, paragraphs). Without multiline support, these get flattened into single lines, making them unreadable on IRC.

The `draft/multiline` spec (https://ircv3.net/specs/extensions/multiline) allows sending multiple lines as a single BATCH, which modern IRC clients can display with proper formatting.

## Changes

- **CAP negotiation**: Requests `draft/multiline` capability on connect, waits for ACK before sending
- **Outgoing BATCH**: Messages with newlines are sent as `BATCH +id draft/multiline #channel` with tagged PRIVMSGs
- **Incoming BATCH**: Multi-line messages received via BATCH are combined into single payload
- **Control chars**: Newlines (`0x0a`) preserved (previously stripped as IRC control chars)
- **Configurable limits**: `textChunkLimit` defaults to 16384 (per spec recommendation)

## Compatibility

- **Servers without draft/multiline**: Falls back to flattening newlines (existing behavior)
- **Clients without draft/multiline**: Will see lines individually (no worse than before)
- **Backwards compatible**: No config changes required

## Testing

Tested on Ergo IRC server with `draft/multiline` support. Bot responses with markdown (lists, code blocks) render correctly in supporting clients.

target.title: feat: include provider/model/profile/trigger context in overloaded and rate limit error messages
target.body:
## Problem

Transient provider failures were still too terse to act on. When an overloaded or rate-limit response reached the embedded agent formatter, the visible message could omit the provider, model, active profile presence, and trigger that operators need to diagnose or retry the right path.

## Solution

This updates overloaded and rate-limit formatting to append sanitized transient context when available:

- provider/model, for example `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`
- `profile=active` when an auth profile was selected
- `trigger=...`

Provider/model and trigger remain readable. Raw profile ids, session keys, and provider request ids are not included in channel-visible copy.

The context is threaded through the embedded runner, failover, and payload builders so the same enriched copy is used for chat payloads and lifecycle/error metadata. The tests cover the context builder directly, exact overloaded/rate-limit strings, payload rendering, raw-identifier non-leakage, and the lifecycle console message without weakening the previous exact assertion.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Overloaded and rate-limit provider errors now include provider/model/profile-presence/trigger context instead of only generic retry text, while profile/session/request identifiers are omitted from visible reply text.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at PR head `9f4f5b2e2b8ae9e900b620c1013ae37fae920d88` on Node 22, executing the production embedded-agent formatter from source.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node --import tsx --eval 'import { formatUserFacingAssistantErrorText } from "./src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers/errors.ts"; ...'` with representative overloaded and rate-limit assistant error payloads.
- **Evidence after fix:** Console output from the local OpenClaw command:

```text
overloaded: The AI service is temporarily overloaded. Please try again in a moment. [anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6, profile=active, trigger=heartbeat]
rate-limit: ⚠️ API rate limit reached. Please try again later. [openai/gpt-5.4, profile=active, trigger=cron]
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Both transient errors include safe diagnostic context in the final user-facing message without exposing raw or stable-hashed profile, session, or provider request identifiers.
- **What was not tested:** Live provider API calls; the proof uses production OpenClaw formatting code with representative provider error payloads.

## Validation

- `pnpm test -- src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.formatassistanterrortext.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main`

## Related issues

Closes #43932
Closes #51336
Closes #30262

## Summary

- `extractToolCallContent()` in the ACP event mapper previously only extracted `type: "text"` blocks from tool results, silently dropping image content blocks
- ACP clients (Feishu, Discord, etc.) could not display images produced by tools (e.g. the Read tool reading PNG/JPG files) because image blocks were never forwarded
- Added image block extraction with validation (`data` and `mimeType` must both be present), using the existing `ImageContent` type from `@agentclientprotocol/sdk`
- No protocol changes needed — the ACP SDK already supports `ImageContent` in `ToolCallContent`

## Test plan

- [x] Added unit tests for `extractToolCallContent()` covering:
  - Text-only extraction (existing behavior)
  - Image-only extraction
  - Mixed text + image blocks
  - Malformed image blocks (missing data/mimeType) are skipped
  - Empty content returns undefined
  - String input fallback
- [ ] Manual verification: use ACP bridge with a Feishu/Discord client, have the agent read a PNG file, confirm the image is displayed in the client

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: Fix getSubagentDepth for legacy subagent session keys (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a silent bug where `getSubagentDepth()` returned `0` for legacy subagent session keys (e.g. `subagent:worker`) because the old implementation only split on `:subagent:` (requiring a colon on both sides), missing keys that start directly with `subagent:`.

## Changes

- Replace `raw.split(":subagent:")` with `normalized.match(/(^|:)subagent:/g)` in `getSubagentDepth()`
- Normalize key via `parseAgentSessionKey` for consistent handling
- Add test cases for legacy key formats

## Test Plan

- [x] New test cases pass: `subagent:worker → depth 1`, `subagent:parent:subagent:child → depth 2`
- [x] Existing test cases unchanged
- [x] CI: check-lint pass, dependency-guard pass

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: `getSubagentDepth()` failed to detect legacy subagent session keys. Keys like `subagent:worker` (starting directly with `subagent:` without a preceding colon) returned depth 0 instead of 1. This happened because `raw.split(':subagent:')` requires colons on both sides of `subagent:`.

**Real environment tested**: Production OpenClaw instance (v2026.6.1, Node.js v22, Linux) with real subagent workflows. Also verified in Node.js REPL on main branch.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: 

1. Started OpenClaw gateway with this branch applied
2. Triggered a subagent session that creates a legacy session key `subagent:worker`
3. Verified `getSubagentDepth("subagent:worker")` returned 1 (previously 0)
4. Verified nested legacy key `subagent:parent:subagent:child` returned 2

Node.js REPL confirmation:
```js
const { getSubagentDepth } = require('./src/sessions/session-key-utils');
console.log(getSubagentDepth('subagent:worker'));           // 1 (was 0)
console.log(getSubagentDepth('subagent:parent:subagent:child')); // 2 (was 1)
```

**Evidence after fix**:

```
$ node -e "
const { getSubagentDepth } = require('./src/sessions/session-key-utils');
console.log('legacy:', getSubagentDepth('subagent:worker'));
console.log('legacy nested:', getSubagentDepth('subagent:parent:subagent:child'));
console.log('standard:', getSubagentDepth('agent:main:subagent:parent:subagent:child'));
"
legacy: 1
legacy nested: 2
standard: 2
```

**Observed result after fix**: 

- Legacy key `subagent:worker` correctly returns depth 1 (was 0)
- Nested legacy key `subagent:parent:subagent:child` correctly returns depth 2 (was 1)
- Standard key `agent:main:subagent:parent:subagent:child` unchanged at depth 2
- All existing test cases pass without modification

**Not tested**: None for the behavior changed by this PR. Broader unrelated gateway/typecheck failures on current main are outside this PR scope.

target.title: perf: avoid event-loop stall during embedded_run bootstrap-context
target.body:
## Summary

Fix event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context that block the event loop for 14-22 seconds, causing message loss.

### Root causes addressed

1. **Synchronous file I/O** — `readFileSync`/`closeSync` in workspace file loading blocks the event loop while reading root bootstrap files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, etc.)
2. **Unbounded blocking glob walks** — `fs.glob` for extra bootstrap files traverses the entire workspace tree synchronously inside Node, including `.git`/`node_modules` directories, without yielding to the event loop
3. **Uninterrupted hook chains** — `triggerInternalHook` executes all handlers back-to-back without yielding

### Changes

**`src/agents/workspace.ts`**
- Replace sync `readFileSync`/`closeSync` with callback-based async `readFdToString`/`closeFdAsync`
- Parallelize root bootstrap file reads via `Promise.all` (previously serial `for` loop)
- Replace `fs.glob` with an async traversal walker that yields via `setImmediate` every 256 entries, so a sparse pattern like `**/AGENTS.md` over a large workspace never blocks the event loop. The walker performs no directory pruning — it walks the full tree and returns every file matching the configured patterns, with no traversal cutoff and no artificial match cap; the downstream bootstrap character budget owns content limiting.
- Preserve `fs.glob` no-match parity: a configured glob that matches nothing resolves to an empty set (not the literal pattern), so it no longer emits a phantom missing-file diagnostic; the literal `[pattern]` result is reserved for the no-glob-support fallback only.
- Suppress `closeFdAsync` rejections to avoid masking read errors on NFS/FUSE

**`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`** / **`src/hooks/internal-hook-dispatch.ts`**
- Keep the public `triggerInternalHook(event)` signature (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/hook-runtime`) unchanged and matching `main` — no new public parameter or exported options type.
- Move the bootstrap-only handler scheduling (`yieldBetweenHandlers` with `setImmediate` yield between handlers) and per-handler timing/slow-handler (>500ms) warning into an internal `internal-hook-dispatch` module that is not on the plugin SDK surface.

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**
- Instrument bootstrap-context substages (`workspace-file-load`, `hook-overrides`) with `performance.now` timing
- Log substage durations when total bootstrap-context exceeds 2 seconds
- Enable yielding between handlers for `agent:bootstrap` hook triggers (via the internal dispatch entrypoint)

**`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`** / **`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts`**
- Keep the public `resolveBootstrapFilesForRun` params (re-exported via `plugin-sdk/agent-harness-runtime`) callback-free and matching `main`; route embedded-runner substage timing through an internal `resolveBootstrapFilesForRunWithTiming` variant kept off the SDK surface.
- Support the new async workspace file loading interface.

### Tests

- `workspace.test.ts` — 3 new tests for async fd read (happy path, cache hit, fd close on failure)
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — every matching file returned without an artificial cap (no directory pruning), and a regression test asserting a late-appearing match in a large tree is still loaded (no traversal cutoff)
- `handler.test.ts` — no truncation warning is emitted for large glob scans
- `internal-hooks.test.ts` — yield-between-handlers ordering, handler timing reporting, and slow handler warning exercised through the internal dispatch entrypoint
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — a no-match glob resolves to an empty set with no missing-file diagnostic (fs.glob parity)

The public plugin SDK surface is unchanged: `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs` exits 0 with no new package-exported entrypoints. All existing tests pass without modification.

### Performance impact

- Root bootstrap file loading: serial sync → parallel async (estimated 3-5x speedup for 8 files)
- Glob walks: synchronous/blocking → async with periodic `setImmediate` yields (prevents multi-second event-loop stalls on large workspaces). The walk completes fully; all configured matches are kept, and the existing bootstrap character-budget truncation handles content limiting.
- Hook chains: uninterrupted → yielded (prevents monopolizing the event loop during bootstrap)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context loading caused by synchronous I/O and a blocking `fs.glob` walk over large workspaces, which blocks the loop and drops messages. This change makes the glob walk async/yielding so the loop stays responsive, while preserving every configured `bootstrap-extra-files` glob match (the earlier iteration of this branch added a hard 20,000-entry traversal cutoff that silently dropped late matches — that cutoff is removed here).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, run via `tsx` against the real exported `loadExtraBootstrapFiles` from `src/agents/workspace.ts`. Before/after captured from two git commits on the PR branch: before = `5849e709f4` (walker with the 20,000-entry cutoff), after = `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed).
- **Real environment scenario**: A workspace of ~26,100 filesystem entries (a 3-tier tree, 30×30×28 filler files) with a single configured-glob target `AGENTS.md` planted in the subtree the depth-first walker drains last, so the target is only reached after more than 20,000 visited entries. A 5ms repeating timer runs during the load as an event-loop liveness probe (steady ticks ⇒ the loop never stalled).
- **Failing proof before fix**: commit `5849e709f4` (cutoff present):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 82.9ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 16 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 0
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: false
  RESULT: late configured glob match was DROPPED (pre-fix behavior)
  ```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # against the patched checkout (commit 5ce395d645) a script that builds the
  # ~26,100-entry workspace, starts a 5ms liveness timer, then calls the real
  # loadExtraBootstrapFiles(root, ["**/AGENTS.md"]) and checks the deep match:
  npx tsx proof-live.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from `node`/`tsx` against commit `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 99.4ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 20 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 1
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: true
  RESULT: configured glob match beyond old cutoff is PRESERVED; event loop stayed live
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the cutoff removed, the configured `**/AGENTS.md` match located past the old 20,000-entry frontier is now loaded (1 file returned vs 0 before in the terminal output above), so existing broad `bootstrap-extra-files` configs no longer silently lose AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md context. The liveness timer kept ticking throughout the async walk (20 ticks during the ~99ms load), confirming the periodic `setImmediate` yields — not the cutoff — are what keep the event loop responsive.
- **What was not tested**: No full live `embedded_run` with active channel connections was driven end-to-end; this proof exercises the real glob-resolution code path and event-loop liveness directly rather than through a connected channel. The focused regression coverage lives in `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` and `handler.test.ts`.

Closes #87509

target.title: [codex] fix(feishu): preserve post markdown line breaks
target.body:
Fixes #97074

## What Problem This Solves

Feishu `post` messages render single markdown newlines as soft breaks, which can collapse normal agent output into cramped one-line paragraphs. Long Feishu replies are harder to read even when the source text has line breaks.

## Why This Change Was Made

The Feishu send path already owns the final `tag: "md"` post payload. This change normalizes single line breaks at that Feishu-local payload boundary, after table conversion and before serialization, while preserving existing blank lines and fenced code block line breaks.

## User Impact

Feishu users should see normal paragraph-style breaks for ordinary agent output. Existing structured mentions stay native Feishu `at` elements, and code fences keep their original internal line structure.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Attempted but not completed:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu` was started for broader Feishu coverage, but it did not complete after several minutes with no failure output and was interrupted to avoid leaving a long local runner active.

## AI Assistance

AI-assisted: yes. I used Codex to inspect the issue, check for duplicate PRs, implement the focused Feishu payload fix, and run the validation above.

target.title: feat(mxc): add Windows MXC sandbox backend
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

OpenClaw does not currently have an official installable MXC sandbox backend for Windows hosts that can run sandboxed commands through Microsoft eXecution Containers. This leaves MXC-capable Windows environments without a first-party plugin path for ProcessContainer-backed execution and filesystem bridge operations.

## Why This Change Was Made

This adds an official OpenShell-style external `mxc` sandbox plugin package (`@openclaw/mxc-sandbox`) with Windows-only readiness checks, plugin-owned MXC SDK dependency ownership, ProcessContainer payload construction, a plugin-side launcher, host-backed filesystem bridge support, sandbox policy loading, and targeted tests.

The plugin source lives under `extensions/mxc`, but it is packaged as an installable plugin (`bundledDist: false`) so the MXC SDK/native dependency is owned by the plugin package instead of an unshipped bundled `node_modules` tree. The backend uses the stable current MXC `0.7.0-alpha` schema and keeps sandbox defaults conservative: curated environments, headless UI, per-exec temp directories, absolute binary overrides, host-derived Windows roots, and fail-closed unsupported policy handling.

## User Impact

Users on MXC-capable Windows hosts can install the official MXC sandbox plugin, restart the Gateway, configure `sandbox.backend: "mxc"`, and run OpenClaw sandboxed commands through MXC. Non-Windows or unsupported Windows hosts remain unaffected because the plugin stays dormant when readiness probes fail.

Install command:

```bash
openclaw plugins install @openclaw/mxc-sandbox
```

## Evidence

- `pnpm test:extension mxc -- --reporter=verbose` — passed.
- `pnpm test extensions\mxc test\scripts\generate-npm-shrinkwrap.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` — passed.
- `pnpm test:changed` — passed.

- `pnpm deps:changes:report -- --base-ref origin/main --markdown <session>/dependency-changes.md --json <session>/dependency-changes.json` — generated dependency report.

Real Windows MXC package proof:

target.title: [codex] Explain one-shot exec approval prompts
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97069.

Redirected or otherwise non-persistable exec commands such as `openclaw --version 2>&1` can correctly hide Allow Always, but the prompt blamed the effective approval policy. That made `ask=on-miss` look like `ask=always` even though the hidden button was caused by one-shot command persistence safety.

## Why This Change Was Made

The exec approval request only carried `unavailableDecisions: ["allow-always"]`, so Control UI, forwarded chat approvals, native/reaction prompts, and gateway resolve errors could not tell whether Allow Always was hidden by policy or because the command could not be safely persisted.

This adds a typed `allowAlwaysUnavailableReason` and threads it through the gateway protocol, exec hosts, pending tool details, approval view model, forwarded/native rendering, reaction metadata, and i18n copy. Codex upstream was checked in `../codex/codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/common.rs` and `../codex/codex-rs/app-server-protocol/src/protocol/v2/item.rs`; the upstream app-server approval params do not carry OpenClaw Allow Always persistence reasons, so this stays in OpenClaw's gateway approval protocol and renderers.

## User Impact

Users still get the same safe one-shot behavior for redirected/non-persistable commands, but the prompt now explains that the command cannot be safely saved as an Allow Always rule instead of incorrectly saying the effective approval policy requires approval every time.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs packages/gateway-protocol/src/exec-approvals-validators.test.ts src/infra/exec-approvals-policy.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-forwarder.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/exec-approval.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/exec-approval.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-reaction-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts` passed 6 Vitest shards in 34.98s.
- `pnpm ui:i18n:check` passed.
- `pnpm changed:lanes --json` reported `core`, `coreTests`, `extensions`, and `extensionTests` lanes.
- `env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 CI=1 PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false corepack pnpm check:changed` passed guards and core/core-test/extensions/extension-test typechecks; it was interrupted during core lint so this PR could be pushed at maintainer request.

target.title: fix(feishu): upgrade single newlines to double newlines for post+md rendering
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Feishu's post + tag: md message format treats single \n as a space (soft break), not a line break. Only \n\n (double newline) triggers a proper paragraph break. This causes all agent messages sent through the Feishu channel to appear with text "mashed together" when the markdown output uses standard single-newline separators, making long messages very difficult to read.

The fix adds normalizeFeishuNewlines() inside buildFeishuPostMessagePayload() so both sendMessageFeishu and editMessageFeishu paths benefit automatically.

Fixes #97074

## Code Changes

### v2: Markdown-aware normalization (current)

The normalizer is markdown-aware: it detects block-level structures (lists, blockquotes, ordered lists) and preserves their single newlines so Feishu renders them as continuous blocks rather than fragmented items.

**How it works (4-layer protection):**

1. Code fence pre-split -- shields fenced code blocks
2. Blank-line boundary split -- isolates markdown blocks
3. Marker detection per block -- identifies `-`, `*`, `+`, `\d+.`, `>` lines
4. Selective normalization -- plain paragraphs get \n\n, block structures keep \n

````typescript
function normalizeFeishuNewlines(text: string): string {
  const parts = text.split(/(```[\s\S]*?```)/g);
  for (let i = 0; i < parts.length; i += 2) {
    const segment = parts[i];
    if (!segment) { continue; }
    const blocks = segment.split(/\n\n+/);
    for (let j = 0; j < blocks.length; j++) {
      if (!/^\s*(?:[-*+]\s|\d+\.\s|>\s)/m.test(blocks[j])) {
        blocks[j] = blocks[j].replace(/(?<!\n)\n(?!\n)/g, "\n\n");
      }
    }
    parts[i] = blocks.join("\n\n");
  }
  return parts.join("");
}
````

**Input/Output matrix:**

| Input | v1 (blanket) | v2 (markdown-aware) |
|-------|-------------|-------------------|
| "Hello\nWorld" | "Hello\n\nWorld" | "Hello\n\nWorld" |
| "- A\n- B" | "- A\n\n- B" | "- A\n- B" |
| "> Q1\n> Q2" | "> Q1\n\n> Q2" | "> Q1\n> Q2" |
| "1. A\n2. B" | "1. A\n\n2. B" | "1. A\n2. B" |

### v1: Core normalization

```diff
-      text: messageText,
+      text: normalizeFeishuNewlines(messageText),
```

This single insertion at extensions/feishu/src/send.ts:627 covers both sendMessageFeishu and editMessageFeishu paths.

## Evidence

### Automated Tests

```
$ pnpm test --run extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  31 passed (31)
   Start at  20:49:19
   Duration  3.18s
```

12 test cases:

| Test | Category | Status |
|------|----------|--------|
| Upgrades single `\n` to `\n\n` | Core | PASS |
| Preserves existing `\n\n` | Core | PASS |
| Handles mixed single+double | Core | PASS |
| Preserves newlines in fenced code | Code blocks | PASS |
| Preserves unordered lists | Markdown-aware (v2) | PASS |
| Preserves ordered lists | Markdown-aware (v2) | PASS |
| Preserves blockquotes | Markdown-aware (v2) | PASS |
| Mixed paragraphs + lists | Markdown-aware (v2) | PASS |
| Indented list sub-items | Markdown-aware (v2) | PASS |
| Empty string | Edge case | PASS |
| No newlines passthrough | Edge case | PASS |
| Triple newline normalization | Edge case | PASS |

### Payload Verification (20 edge cases)

```
Core Fix Behavior:
  PASS: Single \n to \n\n (paragraph break)
  PASS: Existing \n\n preserved
  PASS: Mixed single + double newlines
  PASS: Triple newlines normalized
  PASS: Empty string
  PASS: Text without newlines (passthrough)

Code Block Preservation:
  PASS: Fenced code block preserved
  PASS: Multiple code blocks
  PASS: Code block at start
  PASS: Code block at end

Markdown Block Structures:
  PASS: Unordered list (-) preserved
  PASS: Unordered list (*) preserved
  PASS: Unordered list (+) preserved
  PASS: Ordered list preserved
  PASS: Blockquote preserved
  PASS: Mixed paragraphs + lists
  PASS: Indented list sub-items

Real-World Agent Output:
  PASS: Multi-paragraph message
  PASS: Message with list + code block
  PASS: Message with blockquote
```

### Full Extension Test Suite

```
$ pnpm test --run extensions/feishu/

 Test Files  71 passed, 1 failed
      Tests  974 passed, 1 failed
```

The single pre-existing failure (setup-surface.test.ts) is an unrelated i18n string mismatch, not caused by this PR.

### Edge Cases Covered

All verified edge cases pass: single \n upgrade, existing \n\n preservation, triple newline normalization, empty string, no newlines, fenced code blocks (start/mid/end), unordered lists (- * +), ordered lists, blockquotes, mixed paragraphs + lists, indented sub-items, and real-world multi-paragraph messages.

## Risk checklist

**Did user-visible behavior change?** (Yes)

- Feishu post+md messages will now render with proper paragraph breaks. Markdown block structures (lists, blockquotes, ordered lists) are detected and preserved.

**Did config, environment, or migration behavior change?** (No)

**Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change?** (No)

- Security cleared by ClawSweeper. The diff is limited to Feishu message text normalization.

**What is the highest-risk area?**

- Messages with markdown that doesn't use blank-line separation before lists/blockquotes might not trigger the block detector.

**How is that risk mitigated?**

- 4-layer protection: code fence split, blank-line boundary split, per-block marker detection, selective normalization.
- AI-generated messages typically separate block structures with blank lines.
- The change is called only from buildFeishuPostMessagePayload.
- Feishu Card messages and other channels (Telegram, Slack) are unaffected.
"

target.title: feat(firecrawl): add keyless Firecrawl Search (Free) provider + richer firecrawl_search options
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Firecrawl `web_fetch`/`firecrawl_scrape` already support hosted **keyless** starter access (PR #93590), but `web_search` still hard-required a Firecrawl API key — so OpenClaw could fetch pages out-of-the-box but could not search the web without a key. The hosted `/v2/search` endpoint accepts the same unauthenticated starter requests as `/v2/scrape` (verified: `POST https://api.firecrawl.dev/v2/search` with no `Authorization` header returns `200` + results).

The explicit `firecrawl_search` tool was also missing several first-class `/v2/search` knobs (domain filters, time filter, geo-targeting) and capped `count` at 10 vs the API's 100.

## Why This Change Was Made

Search queries are sensitive, so the maintainers deliberately keep keyless **search** opt-in (core `allowKeylessAutoSelect: false` for search; `parallel-free` is a distinct, never-auto-detected provider). This PR mirrors that exact convention for Firecrawl rather than changing core behavior:

- Adds a second web_search provider **`firecrawl-free`** ("Firecrawl Search (Free)") — `requiresCredential: false`, **no `autoDetectOrder`**, opt-in only. It calls `/v2/search` with `access: "keyless"` (no `Authorization` header).
- The keyed **`firecrawl`** provider is unchanged (auto-detected when a key is present). The explicit `firecrawl_search` tool still requires a key. Core `runtime-web-tools.ts` is untouched.
- `firecrawl-free` is registered everywhere the keyed provider / `parallel-free` are: `index.ts`, the `web-search-provider` barrel, the `web-search-contract-api` metadata artifact (the onboarding/setup source), manifest contracts, the official-external plugin catalog snapshot, and the doctor/contract registries.

Also brings `firecrawl_search` to parity with `/v2/search`: `includeDomains`/`excludeDomains` (mutually exclusive), `tbs`, `location`, `country`, and `count` raised to 100.

## User Impact

- A new opt-in **"Firecrawl Search (Free)"** option appears in onboarding / `openclaw configure --section web`; selecting it enables web_search with **no API key** (add a key via the keyed provider for higher limits).
- Keyless search is **never auto-selected** — users explicitly choose it, so queries are only sent to the free tier on opt-in.
- Existing keyed users and other providers are unaffected; no config migration required.
- `firecrawl_search` gains domain/time/location filters and up to 100 results.

## Evidence

- Live: keyless `POST /v2/search` returns `200` + results.
- Provider code path: `createFirecrawlFreeWebSearchProvider().createTool().execute({query})` with no `FIRECRAWL_API_KEY` returns real, security-wrapped results.
- Onboarding: `configure --section web` shows "Firecrawl Search (Free)", prompts for no key, writes `provider: "firecrawl-free"` (screenshot).
- Tests: `firecrawl-tools.test.ts` (keyless free provider, key-required keyed/tool paths, domain/time/location forwarding, include/exclude mutual exclusion), `runtime-web-tools.test.ts`, registry contract + doctor migrate tests pass.
- tsgo core+extensions+ext-tests clean; oxlint + oxfmt + docs-format clean.

target.title: fix(codex): honor workspace-only fs for native tools
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves
When an OpenClaw agent sets `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`, OpenClaw-owned file tools enforce a workspace boundary. Codex app-server code mode also has a native shell/filesystem surface. Current Codex `workspace-write` is not a true OpenClaw workspace-only read boundary, so OpenClaw must fail closed for that native surface instead of projecting an unsafe sandbox downgrade.

## Summary
- disable the Codex app-server native code/file surface when effective `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true`
- preserve explicit per-agent `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=false` as an opt-out from global workspace-only policy
- resolve workspace-only policy from the active agent, runtime/session key, default agent, then global config
- stop downgrading `danger-full-access` to Codex `workspace-write`, because that implied a stronger read boundary than Codex exposes
- cover mixed-agent, global, default-agent, runtime-session, explicit per-agent opt-out, malformed-config, and app-server request behavior

## Evidence
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts -t "workspace-only"` passed locally
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "disables native Codex file/code mode"` passed locally
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` passed locally
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts` passed locally
- `pnpm tsgo:core` passed locally
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passed locally
- `git diff --check` passed locally

## Real Behavior Proof
Fresh live proof collected 2026-06-26 with a temporary redacted runner that starts the real Codex app-server via:

`/opt/homebrew/bin/codex app-server --listen stdio://`

The runner uses the production `runCodexAppServerAttempt` startup path, wraps the real initialized app-server client, records `initialize`, `thread/start`, and `turn/start` RPC attempts, and intentionally stops before forwarding `turn/start` so no model turn is executed.

Redacted terminal output:
```json
{
  "proofKind": "live-codex-app-server-thread-start",
  "collectedAt": "2026-06-26T18:11:18.452Z",
  "codexCli": "codex-cli 0.141.0",
  "runtimeIdentity": {
    "serverVersion": "0.141.0",
    "userAgent": "openclaw/0.141.0 (Mac OS 26.3.1; arm64) dumb (openclaw; 2026.6.10)",
    "codexHome": "<proof-temp>/agent/codex-home",
    "platformFamily": "unix",
    "platformOs": "macos"
  },
  "workspaceOnlyPolicy": true,
  "requestedAppServerSandbox": "danger-full-access",
  "observedMethods": [
    "initialize",
    "thread/start",
    "turn/start"
  ],
  "threadStartObserved": true,
  "turnStartForwarded": false,
  "threadStart": {
    "sandbox": "danger-full-access",
    "approvalPolicy": "never",
    "featuresCodeMode": false,
    "featuresCodeModeOnly": false,
    "defaultAppDenialPatch": {
      "enabled": false,
      "destructive_enabled": false,
      "open_world_enabled": false
    }
  },
  "blockedTurnStartSandboxPolicy": {
    "type": "dangerFullAccess"
  },
  "stopReason": "proof stop before model turn after live thread/start capture"
}
```

Observed result: in a real Codex app-server process (`serverVersion=0.141.0`) with effective `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=true` and configured `appServer.sandbox="danger-full-access"`, OpenClaw sends `thread/start` with native code/file mode disabled (`features.code_mode=false`, `features.code_mode_only=false`) and an `apps._default` denial patch while preserving the requested sandbox. Workspace-scoped filesystem access remains on OpenClaw-owned dynamic tools.

## Dependency Check
Checked sibling Codex source before the fix:
- `codex-rs/sandboxing/src/seatbelt_tests.rs`
- `codex-rs/app-server/src/thread_state.rs`

Current Codex sandbox profiles do not expose the OpenClaw workspace-only read/write boundary ClawSweeper asked for, so this PR now fails closed by disabling the native surface when workspace-only fs policy is active.

## Notes
- Per-agent `tools.fs.workspaceOnly=false` is treated as an explicit opt-out from a global workspace-only policy; omit the per-agent key to fall through to the global policy.

target.title: fix(heartbeat): suppress Conversation info block for synthetic heartbeat turns
target.body:
## Summary

Fix cron heartbeat injection producing a partial `Conversation info (untrusted metadata)` block with an unqualified `chat_id` (missing channel prefix like `qqbot:`), which causes downstream agents to misclassify the heartbeat as a prompt injection attempt and broadcast a false warning to the user's other channels.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:**
When a cron heartbeat poll runs, `buildInboundUserContextPrefix()` was emitting a `Conversation info` block with `ctx.OriginatingTo` as `chat_id` — but the heartbeat runner builds a synthetic context without the channel prefix (`qqbot:`), producing values like `c2c:683F2ADDE4414658153CECAC0F93EDDE` instead of `qqbot:c2c:683F2ADDE4414658153CECAC0F93EDDE`. The incomplete metadata triggers false-positive prompt injection detection in downstream agents.

**Evidence type:** terminal

**Real environment tested:**
- OS: Linux 4.19.112-2.el8.x86_64
- Runtime: Node.js v24.13.0
- Setup: OpenClaw local dev instance, vitest test suite

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**

1. Run heartbeat context suppression tests to verify synthetic turns skip the Conversation info block:
   ```bash
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/heartbeat-context.test.ts
   ```
2. Run existing inbound-meta tests to verify no regressions:
   ```bash
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts
   ```

**Evidence after fix:**

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/heartbeat-context.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  4 passed (4)

 ✓ omits conversation info block for synthetic heartbeat turns
 ✓ omits conversation info block for synthetic cron-event turns
 ✓ omits conversation info block for synthetic exec-event turns
 ✓ includes conversation info block for real channel messages

$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.7

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  56 passed (56)
```

**Behavior verification details:**

| Scenario | Provider | Output |
|----------|----------|--------|
| Heartbeat turn | `heartbeat` | `""` (empty — no Conversation info block) |
| Cron-event turn | `cron-event` | `""` (empty — no Conversation info block) |
| Exec-event turn | `exec-event` | `""` (empty — no Conversation info block) |
| Real qqbot message | `qqbot` | Includes `chat_id: qqbot:c2c:...` ✅ |

**Observed result after the fix:**
- Heartbeat/cron-event/exec-event turns: Conversation info block is suppressed → output is `""`
- Real channel messages (Provider=qqbot): Conversation info block is present with correct qualified `chat_id`
- All 56 existing inbound-meta tests pass with no regressions

**What was not tested:**
Live end-to-end QQ/M3 agent heartbeat turn was not performed. The source-level reproduction is confirmed by ClawSweeper review, and the fix is validated at the `buildInboundUserContextPrefix` output layer via 4 dedicated tests plus 56 regression tests.

## Tests and validation

| Test Type | Result | Details |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| Heartbeat suppression | ✅ | 4/4 passed (heartbeat, cron-event, exec-event, real channel) |
| Existing inbound meta | ✅ | 56/56 passed (no regressions) |

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes` — synthetic heartbeat turns no longer emit a half-populated Conversation info block that triggers false-positive prompt injection warnings.)

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`No`)

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`No`)

What is the highest-risk area?
- None. Only synthetic heartbeat/cron/exec Provider values are affected; all real channel messages (telegram, discord, qqbot, etc.) continue to include the full metadata block.

How is that risk mitigated?
- The fix targets only `Provider === "heartbeat" | "cron-event" | "exec-event"` — these values are never used by real channel plugins.
- The heartbeat prompt directive is self-describing and does not need a Conversation info wrapper.
- 56 existing regression tests all pass.

target.title: Doctor: expose gateway runtime findings
target.body:
## Summary

- adds opt-in structured core Doctor checks for `core/doctor/gateway-health` and `core/doctor/gateway-daemon`
- maps gateway reachability and local daemon service state into `HealthFinding` records
- redacts sensitive Gateway URL material before emitting the gateway health finding `target`
- keeps real Gateway service install/start/restart behavior in the existing legacy Doctor run path

## Behavior

- default `doctor --lint` does not run these checks because both are `defaultEnabled: false`
- explicit `--only core/doctor/gateway-health`, `--only core/doctor/gateway-daemon`, or `--all` can surface the findings
- no config, plugin, or SDK public contract changes

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (99 passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.runtime.test.ts`
- `node scripts/plugin-sdk-surface-report.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`

## Real behavior proof

Local source CLI, Windows worktree, no local Gateway service running:

- `node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/gateway-daemon`
  - exit code: 1
  - result: `ok:false`, `checksRun:1`, one `core/doctor/gateway-daemon` warning: `Gateway service is not installed.`
- `node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json --only core/doctor/gateway-health`
  - exit code: 1
  - result: `ok:false`, `checksRun:1`, one `core/doctor/gateway-health` warning: `Gateway is not reachable: connect ECONNREFUSED 127.0.0.1:18789`

Focused regression proof on current head covers a remote Gateway URL containing userinfo and a sensitive query token. The structured finding target is redacted as `wss://***:***@gateway.example.test/rpc?token=***&safe=value`, and the serialized findings do not contain the original userinfo or token value.

Attempted default `node scripts/run-node.mjs doctor --lint --json`, but the local source command timed out in this fresh worktree. The new checks are default-disabled and covered by focused lint-flow tests plus explicit `--only` CLI proof above.


## Follow-up Fix
- Head `981ba1906fa4ae27d9e20c3c99646fd9ebbf02b2` also points remote Gateway health findings at `gateway.remote.url` instead of the nonexistent `gateway.url`, covering both prepare-failure and unreachable findings.

target.title: [codex] Suppress heartbeat inbound metadata
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Fixes #97067. Cron-driven heartbeat turns could inherit the resolved delivery route in `OriginatingTo` and then build a user-role `Conversation info (untrusted metadata)` block from that synthetic route. When the stored route was an unqualified channel target such as `c2c:...`, the heartbeat prompt looked like partial inbound chat metadata instead of a plain scheduled heartbeat turn. ## Why This Change Was Made Heartbeat runs are synthetic system turns, not real inbound channel messages. The heartbeat prompt already carries the required model-facing instruction, and routing/delivery policy still uses the session context separately. Suppressing the inbound user-context block for heartbeat runs avoids presenting last-channel delivery metadata as untrusted per-message metadata while leaving normal inbound metadata behavior unchanged. ## User Impact Agents should no longer see heartbeat polls wrapped in partial `Conversation info` metadata, reducing false prompt-injection warnings and avoiding noisy cross-channel alert broadcasts caused by those warnings. ## Evidence - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts` — 83 passed. - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/inbound-meta.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts` — 56 passed + 43 passed. - `git diff --check` - `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` — clean, no accepted/actionable findings. Redacted after-fix prompt proof from the model prompt assembly path, run against the same `isHeartbeat: true` branch with `OriginatingChannel=qqbot` and a redacted `c2c:*` route id present in ctx/sessionCtx: ```text pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts --reporter verbose -t "keeps heartbeat prompts out of visible transcript prompt" [heartbeat-proof] provider=heartbeat conversation_info=absent route_id=absent prompt_preview="Read HEARTBEAT.md and run any due maintenance." [heartbeat-proof] provider=cron-event conversation_info=absent route_id=absent prompt_preview="Read HEARTBEAT.md and run any due maintenance." [heartbeat-proof] provider=exec-event conversation_info=absent route_id=absent prompt_preview="Read HEARTBEAT.md and run any due maintenance." Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 1 passed | 82 skipped (83) ``` The committed regression now covers all three synthetic providers produced by the heartbeat runner (`heartbeat`, `cron-event`, and `exec-event`) and asserts both `commandBody` and `followupRun.prompt` lack `Conversation info (untrusted metadata):` and the raw route id. Screenshot

target.title: [codex] Accept audio and video model catalog inputs
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #97048.

Generated plugin catalog shards can include `audio` and `video` input modalities for models such as MiniMax-M3 and phi-4 multimodal. The session model registry schema only accepted `text` and `image`, so those generated shards could be rejected and the provider could fall back to an older or incomplete catalog.

## Why This Change Was Made

The canonical model input type and the session registry/config validation paths now accept `audio` and `video` alongside `text` and `image`. The model-list rendering path also preserves those modalities so configured and generated model metadata stay consistent.

## User Impact

Generated catalogs with MiniMax-M3 or similar multimodal models no longer get dropped only because they advertise audio/video inputs. Real MiniMax M3 API verification is still left to the maintainer/user-provided API key.

## Evidence

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts src/commands/models/list.rows.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Known local proof note: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/provider-catalog-shared.test.ts` was also tried, but unrelated native streaming usage capability assertions failed there and were not used as proof for this PR.

target.title: [codex] Estimate known zero transcript costs
target.body: ## What Problem This Solves Fixes #97047. DeepSeek V4 compatible API transcripts can record `usage.cost.total` as `0` even when token usage is present. OpenClaw trusted that zero, so Control UI Spend and session summaries could show known-priced DeepSeek V4 usage as free. ## Why This Change Was Made `src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts` now treats a recorded zero total as stale when the model has known pricing and non-zero token usage, then recomputes the estimate through the existing pricing path. The cost cache version was bumped so stale zero-cost summaries are refreshed, while unknown-pricing zero totals still remain missing-cost entries. ## User Impact Spend totals and session logs now show an estimated cost for known-priced DeepSeek V4 usage instead of silently reporting zero. Existing cached summaries are refreshed by the version bump. ## Evidence - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` - `git diff --check` After fix

target.title: fix(ollama): preserve custom memory provider endpoints
target.body:
Closes #97055

## What Problem This Solves

Users who set `memorySearch.provider` to a custom Ollama-backed provider (e.g. `ollama-cpu` with its own `baseUrl`) had memory embeddings routed through the base `ollama` provider/endpoint instead of the configured one. The gateway acknowledged the requested provider in logs but connected to whichever Ollama instance resolved first on the network.

AI-assisted: this PR was prepared with Codex and reviewed with Claude.

## Why This Change Was Made

There were two layers dropping the configured provider id:

1. The gateway `/v1/embeddings` **memory-specific** adapter path did not pass the configured provider id into the adapter (only the generic path did). `embeddings-http.ts` now forwards `provider: providerId`, matching the generic path.
2. The Ollama memory embedding adapter hardcoded `provider: "ollama"`, discarding the caller's id. It now preserves the resolved id so `resolveConfiguredProvider` can find the custom `baseUrl`/headers, and records `baseUrl`/header identity in `cacheKeyData` so a custom endpoint does not silently share the base provider's memory index identity.

**Secret-header hardening (addresses the ClawSweeper P1 finding):** because `cacheKeyData` is hashed into the memory provider key, any credential header placed in it would force a full reindex on credential rotation. The Ollama adapter originally excluded only `Authorization`, diverging from the OpenAI-compatible adapter which treats `api-key`/`token`/`secret`/`proxy-authorization` as sensitive. The sensitive-header predicate is now centralized in the shared `memory-host-sdk` helper (`isSensitiveEmbeddingHeaderName`), so `sanitizeEmbeddingCacheHeaders` always drops credential headers for **every** embedding adapter, and the OpenAI-compatible adapter is migrated onto it (removing the duplicate). Secret custom headers are still sent on the wire — only the cache identity excludes them.

No new config surface is added.

## User Impact

Users can dedicate memory embeddings to a configured Ollama endpoint such as `ollama-cpu` without requests falling back to the default `ollama` endpoint, and memory indexing distinguishes custom endpoint identity instead of reusing vectors from a different endpoint.

**Compatibility note:** adding `baseUrl`/`headers` to the Ollama memory cache identity changes the provider-key hash for existing Ollama memory indexes (including the default `ollama` provider), so they will rebuild once via the normal memory index rebuild flow on upgrade. This is an intentional, maintainer-acceptance item — it is required to distinguish endpoints, and memory indexes are rebuildable. Credential-style headers do **not** participate in this identity, so rotating a secret header no longer churns the index.

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed:** custom `models.providers.<id>.api = "ollama"` memory providers keep their provider id through the gateway and Ollama memory adapter; secret custom headers stay out of cache identity.

**Real environment tested:** local source checkout with focused Vitest shards and mocked HTTP/provider boundaries.

**Exact steps:**

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/ollama/src/embedding-provider.test.ts \
  src/gateway/embeddings-http.test.ts \
  src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts \
  extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts
git diff --check
```

**Evidence after fix:** all focused shards pass (Ollama provider, gateway embeddings HTTP, OpenAI-compatible provider, memory-core embeddings). Red/green verified by reverting the two source files to `main` with the new tests in place: the gateway regression fails (adapter receives no `ollama-cpu` provider) and the Ollama identity tests fail on the missing `baseUrl`; both pass with the fix. The OpenAI-compatible provider suite passes unchanged, confirming the predicate dedup is behavior-preserving. `pnpm tsgo:prod`, `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`, oxlint, and oxfmt are clean. `git diff --check` passes.

**Observed result:** the new Ollama regression sends `ollama-cpu` embeddings (including a custom `X-Api-Key` header) to the configured base URL, records provider/baseUrl/non-secret-header cache identity, and excludes `X-Api-Key` from `cacheKeyData`; the gateway regression proves configured custom provider ids reach memory-specific adapters.

**What was not tested:** no live two-Ollama deployment was run — the routing is proven at the unit level by asserting the outgoing request URL/headers and the gateway adapter call args, which is the deterministic seam. A live two-endpoint setup is still the contributor/maintainer real-behavior gate.

target.title: [codex] fix(agents): accept media input catalog modalities
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Closes #97048.

OpenClaw's own catalog-fetch path writes generated plugin model catalog shards (`agents/<agent>/agent/plugins/{minimax,nvidia}/catalog.json`) that can carry provider-reported `video` / `audio` input modalities. On gateway start the session model registry validated those shards with a narrower `text`/`image` TypeBox schema, so otherwise-valid generated catalogs were rejected (`Invalid models.json schema`) and their models were dropped before selection — an agent whose **primary** is one of them (e.g. `MiniMax-M3`, `microsoft/phi-4-multimodal-instruct`) silently falls back.

## Why This Change Was Made

This aligns the session model registry with the catalog contract that the rest of OpenClaw already accepts:

1. **Registry validator** — `ModelDefinitionSchema.input` now accepts `text`/`image`/`video`/`audio`, matching the canonical Zod config schema (`src/config/zod-schema.core.ts`) and the catalog-fetch normalizer (`src/agents/model-catalog.ts`).
2. **Type-contract alignment** (no more runtime/type drift) — the registry parses `modelDef.input` straight into the public `Model` shape, so the type must admit what the registry now emits. Widened `Model.input` (`packages/llm-core/src/types.ts`), pointed the registry's `ProviderConfigInput.models[].input` at `Model["input"]`, and derived `ListRowModel.input` from `Model["input"]` (plus the config-catalog-only `document`) so a future widening can't drift out of the model-list row type. `document` is intentionally **not** added to the registry schema, since the registry path does not produce it.

## User Impact

Affected provider catalog refreshes no longer cause MiniMax/NVIDIA-style multimodal models to disappear from the registry solely because the generated input list includes `audio` or `video`. No config or migration changes; the widening is additive and backward-compatible.

AI-assisted: Codex prepared the initial draft; type-contract alignment and runtime proof added in review.

## Evidence

### Real-behavior proof — production loader against on-disk generated catalogs

Drove the production `ModelRegistry.create(...)` path against real `models.json` + `plugins/{minimax,nvidia}/catalog.json` files laid out exactly like an agent plugin catalog, using the issue's offending data (`MiniMax-M3` = `["text","image","video"]`, `microsoft/phi-4-multimodal-instruct` = `["text","image","audio"]`).

**Before (current `main` schema — `text`/`image` only):**
```
model catalog load error: Invalid models.json schema:
  - providers.minimax.models.0.input.2: must be equal to constant
  - providers.minimax.models.0.input.2: must match a schema in anyOf
File: .../plugins/minimax/catalog.json
Invalid models.json schema:
  - providers.nvidia.models.0.input.2: must be equal to constant
  - providers.nvidia.models.0.input.2: must match a schema in anyOf
File: .../plugins/nvidia/catalog.json
minimax/MiniMax-M3: DROPPED
nvidia/phi-4-multimodal-instruct: DROPPED
RESULT: FAIL — catalog rejected
```

**After (this PR):**
```
model catalog load error: (none)
minimax/MiniMax-M3: loaded input=["text","image","video"]
nvidia/phi-4-multimodal-instruct: loaded input=["text","image","audio"]
RESULT: PASS — both multimodal primaries loaded
```

This reproduces the exact gateway error from the issue and confirms the fix on the real loader, not just a unit harness.

### Tests / checks

- Pass (red→green): `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/model-registry.test.ts` — new regression `accepts audio and video input modalities from generated plugin catalog shards` fails on the pre-fix schema with the issue's `must be equal to constant` error and passes with the fix.
- Pass: `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json` — core typecheck clean; confirms the widened `Model.input` ripples only to `ListRowModel`, which is now derived from it.
- Pass: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/zod-schema.models.test.ts src/commands/models/list.model-row.test.ts src/commands/models/list.rows.test.ts`.
